**Reference Documents for Music Enthusiasts**

***Frequently Asked Questions***

Why do I only see one album when using the year slider?

- When the circles are stacked on top of each other in the “Number One Albums” tab, only the top album from that year will appear. To see top albums across a range of years, drag the circles apart, as shown below. 

![][image1]

Why don’t I see all artists or albums in the dropdown lists?

- The results shown in this software only represent the data that has been inputted by the author. If a particular album from an artist is not included, it is not one of the author’s favorite albums. 

What is an ‘EP’ or ‘Live Album’?

- An ‘EP’ is a musical release containing more songs than a ‘single’ but fewer than a full-length album. A live album is a recording of songs performed in front of a live audience. Because these are not official albums, they can be excluded if you are not interested in viewing them. 

How can I interpret the graphs in the ‘Artist Comparison’ tab?

- The x-axis represents the year the album was released whereas the y-axis represents the albums ranking (1-10). This allows you to compare how albums from two different artists differ in ranking and year released. 

Can I see albums with ratings lower than 7?

- No. 

Why do some years or artists show more results than others?

- It depends on the albums inputted by the author. There may be more albums in a given year if the author liked more albums during that year. 

[image1]: <data:image/png;base64,>
